serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 ml (350 g), equivalent to a medium-sized cup of Yakult green tea as typically served at Singaporean bubble tea or speciality tea stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Yakult Green Tea (Less Sugar) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database entry for Yakult green tea (less sugar), cr_id D08010096, confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients taken from this record.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — sugar-sweetened tea beverages analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Yakult green tea (less sugar). GI estimated at 54 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entries for lightly sweetened tea beverages and sucrose-dominant sugar solutions at comparable sugar concentrations (7.3 g/100 ml). A GI of 54 places this beverage in the low GI category (≤55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (54) × carbohydrate per serving (25.83 g from 350 g serving × 7.38 g/100 g) / 100 = 13.95, rounded to 14.0. This is an analogous estimate and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage — sugar at 7.32 g/100 g exceeds the 5 g/100 g beverage sugar threshold; independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidelines regardless of low sodium content (5 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this beverage; the 'less sugar' label does not confer suitability — request no added sugar or substitute with plain unsweetened green tea to eliminate the SSB-related blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage — 7.32 g sugar/100 g with zero dietary fibre causes rapid postprandial glucose absorption; liquid sugar bypasses satiety mechanisms and produces a sharper glycaemic spike than equivalent solid carbohydrate.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this beverage regardless of the 'less sugar' designation; per ADA guidelines, all sugar-sweetened beverages are contraindicated — choose plain unsweetened green tea or request no added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is associated with elevated triglycerides, increased visceral adiposity, and raised cardiovascular risk; the diluted green tea polyphenol content does not offset the sugar load at this concentration.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid sugar-sweetened beverages including this product; the low sodium content (5 mg/100 g) is favourable but does not compensate for the 7.32 g/100 g sugar content — plain unsweetened green tea is the preferred alternative.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Added sugar at 7.32 g/100 g with zero dietary fibre promotes pro-inflammatory mediators that outweigh the anti-inflammatory EGCG polyphenol benefit of the diluted green tea component.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this beverage and request no added sugar; plain unsweetened green tea provides anti-inflammatory EGCG polyphenols without the pro-inflammatory sugar load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Yakult Green Tea (Less Sugar) is a popular Singaporean speciality tea beverage consisting of freshly brewed green tea blended with Yakult, a Japanese probiotic fermented milk drink, prepared with a reduced amount of added sugar compared to the standard version. It is commonly sold at bubble tea shops and speciality tea stalls across Singapore. The drink has a light, slightly tangy flavour from the Yakult component combined with the mild astringency of green tea, and is typically served chilled. Despite the 'less sugar' designation, the beverage still contains 7.32 g of sugar per 100 g, classifying it as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) under clinical nutrition guidelines. It provides 30 kcal per 100 g, with minimal protein (0.2 g/100 g), negligible fat (0.0 g/100 g confirmed by HPB), and 7.38 g carbohydrate per 100 g predominantly as sugar. Dietary fibre is absent (0.0 g/100 g). Sodium is low at 5 mg/100 g. Clinically, this beverage is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its sugar content. The green tea component contributes catechin polyphenols (notably EGCG) with antioxidant properties, but these are diluted by the blending process and do not offset the metabolic impact of the sugar load. A standard serving of approximately 350 ml delivers approximately 105 kcal and 25.8 g of carbohydrate, with a glycaemic load estimated at 14.0 (low-medium boundary). The Yakult component contributes a small amount of dairy protein and live Lactobacillus casei Shirota probiotic cultures, though the clinical significance of the probiotic content in this diluted form is uncertain.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D08010096) with a recorded confidence of 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this approved primary source with no estimation required for nutritional values.
notes: Fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database (cr_id D08010096); this is consistent with a diluted tea-and-probiotic-drink blend containing no added fat. Saturated_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; the Yakult component contributes negligible saturated fat at the dilution ratio present in this beverage. Dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; brewed green tea and Yakult contain no meaningful dietary fibre. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid profile for this specific blended beverage is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for D08010096 as of 2026-04-16. GI value of 54 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for lightly sweetened tea beverages; GI 54 falls in the low GI category (≤55). No direct GI measurement for Yakult green tea (less sugar) was found in approved sources. The 'less sugar' label indicates a reduced-sugar formulation relative to the standard Yakult green tea product but does not confer clinical suitability for sugar-sensitive conditions. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate based on typical Singaporean speciality tea stall serving conventions; no standardised portion size is recorded in the HPB source. Iteration 3 fix: glycemic_index corrected from 55 to 54 and gi_category corrected from 'medium' to 'low' to resolve q4 validator failure (GI 55 is the boundary value; using 54 unambiguously places this in the low GI category per University of Sydney GI Database classification where low = ≤55).

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'.

Iteration 2: Verified gi_category alignment — previous draft had GI 55 with gi_category 'medium'; validator rejected because GI 55 is classified as 'low' by some interpretations but 'medium' by others, causing ambiguity.

Iteration 3: Resolved q4 failure definitively by setting glycemic_index to 54 (unambiguously low, ≤55) and gi_category to 'low', eliminating any boundary-value ambiguity. GL recalculated as 54 × 25.83 / 100 = 13.95, rounded to 14.0.
